betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6High Pressure Steam§f:
High-Pressure Steam (high_pressure_steam fluid) is the optimal input for the Steam Turbine. It is the densest, hottest form of steam in NC's fluid system and yields the most RF per mB.

§6Production Paths (FissionHeatingRecipes.java:14-15)§f:
High-Pressure Steam is produced by the Fission Heater (FissionHeatingRecipes.java:14-15):
- §bWater → High-Pressure Steam§f: 1mB water → 4mB HP steam, 64 RF/mB heat input
- §bPreheated Water → High-Pressure Steam§f: 1mB → 4mB HP steam, 32 RF/mB heat input (half the cost)

§6Preheated Water Production (HeatExchangerRecipes)§f:
Preheated Water comes from a §bHeat Exchanger§f multiblock that captures waste heat from the reactor's NaK coolant loop. The full chain is:
- Reactor heat → NaK (cold) → Fission Heater → NaK (hot) → Heat Exchanger → Preheated Water → Fission Heater → High-Pressure Steam → Turbine → RF + Exhaust Steam

§6Why High-Pressure Steam?§f:
Compared to regular steam, High-Pressure Steam:
- Yields 4× the RF per mB of input water
- Has a 4× higher expansion rate (4mB HP steam → 4mB exhaust steam vs 1mB steam → 2mB low-quality steam)
- Allows turbines to operate at maximum efficiency

§6Exhaust Steam Recycling§f:
After the Turbine processes HP steam, the output is §bExhaust Steam§f (4mB per 1mB HP steam). Exhaust Steam can be:
- Recirculated back through the Heat Exchanger to recover some heat
- Condensed back into Water in a Condenser multiblock
- Treated as waste and vented (low efficiency)

§6Turbine Power (TurbineRecipes.java:17)§f:
The Turbine converts HP steam at rate turbine_power_per_mb[0] (the highest of the three input tiers). The actual RF/mB is configurable in NCConfig.

§6Direct Descendants of High-Pressure Steam (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The quest has no specific task item — it's a description of the high-pressure steam production chain. The flow is fully defined by the Fission Heater, Heat Exchanger, and Turbine multiblocks.

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30050 (Steam Turbine) — without a turbine, there's no consumer for the high-pressure steam.

§eTip§f: Always preheat water before sending it to the Fission Heater. The preheated-water path uses half the heat per mB of High-Pressure Steam produced, doubling your effective RF output.

§eTip§f: For very large reactors, add a Condenser to recirculate Exhaust Steam back to Water. This reduces the water input by ~75% and improves overall efficiency.

§cWarning§f: High-Pressure Steam at 4× expansion can overpressure pipes. Use reinforced fluid pipes (e.g., Mekanism's Ultimate Mechanical Pipe) to avoid burst events.
